finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_18.md:87 (Exchange 2: Nate redirects with 'What are you reading?' instead of conceding); chapters/chapter_18.md:245 (Exchange 3: Sarah opens with workday stress—parent calling during quiz—rather than diagnosing Nate); chapters/chapter_18.md:263-269 (Exchange 3b: switch plate ending revised—Nate self-corrects before Sarah points it out, 'I know'/'Do you'/'Now I do' ladder removed); chapters/chapter_18.md:373 (Exchange 4: kept as the single allowed correction/concession ending on 'I know'/'All right')
finding_results.notes: Revised two of the four cited exchanges. Exchange 2 (fake sleeping, lines 81-95): replaced Nate's apology-concession with his style-bible evasion pattern (asks a question about the other person); Sarah calls the deflection and the exchange ends unresolved rather than on a neat concession beat. Exchange 3 (masonry anchors + switch plate, lines 245-269): masonry anchors section now opens with Sarah venting about a parent calling her classroom phone during a quiz, giving her pressure originating in her own workday rather than Nate's oddity; the switch plate section replaces the 'I know'/'Do you'/'Now I do' verbal correction ladder with Nate noticing the off-true plate and self-correcting (per his style-bible self-correction tendency) before Sarah steps in with a practical 'Other way.' Exchange 1 (closet/weather stripping, lines 33-61) was already alive and not correction/concession—kept. Exchange 4 (window/dinner, lines 347-375) retained as the one permitted correction/concession resolution. Acceptance test satisfied: (1) only one exchange resolves as Sarah-correction/Nate-concession; (2) Exchange 3 gives Sarah a line of pressure from her own day; (3) Exchange 2 gives Nate an evasion form other than 'I know'/'Okay'/'Probably'/'Now I do.'

summary: Single finding (chapter_18_F03) addressed by revising two of four cited domestic exchanges to break the repeated Sarah-correction/Nate-concession pattern. Exchange 2 now uses Nate's evasion style (redirecting via a question about Sarah) instead of apology-concession. Exchange 3 gives Sarah her own workday pressure as opening leverage and replaces the switch plate's verbal correction ladder with Nate self-correcting and Sarah stepping in physically. One exchange kept as the permitted single correction/concession resolution. All character voice signatures (Sarah's precision, Nate's guardedness and evasion pattern) preserved. No sanitization, no polish upgrades, no narrator commentary admiring dialogue introduced.
